identitySentence: VRBA Law, PLLC is a Denton criminal defense firm on West Hickory Street where attorneys Rudy "Glynn" Vrba and Lindsey Pruitt devote their entire practice to defending people charged with crimes in Denton County.

concept.headline: Charged in Denton County? Talk to a criminal defense lawyer who handles nothing else.
concept.subheadline: An arrest is frightening, and the hours after it are confusing. VRBA Law, PLLC practices 100% criminal defense from downtown Denton, and a confidential consultation can help you understand where you stand and what comes next.
credibilityPoints: A practice devoted 100% to criminal defense
Attorney Lindsey Pruitt, licensed to practice law since 2008
Member of the Denton County Criminal Defense Lawyers Association
Office at 121 W Hickory St #115 in downtown Denton

sections.title: Practice focus
sections.body: VRBA Law, PLLC defends people facing DWI/DUI, assault, domestic and family violence, drug possession, theft, felony, and misdemeanor charges. The firm also handles expunctions and non-disclosures for those looking to clear a record, as well as traffic ticket defense.

sections.title: Denton roots
sections.body: The firm is based at 121 W Hickory St #115 in downtown Denton and positions its entire practice around Denton and Denton County, with membership in the Denton County Criminal Defense Lawyers Association.

sections.title: What to expect
sections.body: In a consultation, the firm will ask about the charge, the circumstances of the arrest, and any upcoming court dates. You will get a straightforward assessment of the situation, not pressure or promises.

outreachDraft.subject: Proof matters more than promises: 15 days, 1 demo, and a Lead Capturer that can pay for itself before you ever have to.
outreachDraft.body:
DRAFT — NOT SENT. Review and authorize before any outreach.

To the team at VRBA Law, PLLC,

Your practice-areas page says it plainly: the firm is 100% criminal defense, based at 121 W Hickory St #115 in downtown Denton. That kind of focus is exactly why we built something specific for you.

Your website already does a lot right: clear practice pages for every charge type, real attorney bios, client testimonials, and a prominent phone number. What it does not have is any way to book a consultation online, so intake depends entirely on phone calls and a contact form. When someone is arrested on a Friday night for DWI or a family violence allegation, they search immediately, and an inquiry sent at midnight waits until someone is back at a desk. For a practice built on urgent, time-sensitive matters, that gap is where potential clients quietly move on to the next firm on the list.

The Lead Capturer closes that gap, and it was built around your firm specifically. We wrote a custom identity sentence for VRBA Law: "VRBA Law, PLLC is a Denton criminal defense firm on West Hickory Street where attorneys Rudy \"Glynn\" Vrba and Lindsey Pruitt devote their entire practice to defending people charged with crimes in Denton County." We paired it with a custom contact form written for your actual practice areas, from DWI and drug possession to assault, theft, expunctions, and traffic tickets.

Here is how it works:
- Every inquiry is emailed directly to the firm's inbox.
- The potential client automatically receives a topical response specific to their message, so a DWI inquiry gets a DWI-relevant reply rather than a generic acknowledgment.
- That response presents your available consultation times.
- A $50 reservation deposit confirms the appointment, so a held time slot belongs to someone who intends to keep it.

We are not asking you to take any of this on faith, and we will not quote revenue projections we cannot support. We offer a 15-day proof period so you can test the complete experience yourself, end to end, before deciding anything.

How to test your Lead Capturer (15 minutes):
1. Submit a clearly labeled TEST inquiry using a customer email you control.
2. Do not enter confidential client information.
3. Confirm the firm inbox receives a real email.
4. Confirm the notification contains every displayed form field.
5. Confirm the subject identifies the firm and the likely matter type.
6. Confirm the Reply-To contains the submitted customer email.
7. Confirm the system automatically generates a response specific to your TEST inquiry.
8. Confirm the response is sent to the customer email without any staff action.
9. Confirm the customer email actually receives it.
10. Confirm it addresses the actual inquiry topic you submitted.
11. Confirm genuine available consultation times are presented.
12. Select a time.
13. Confirm the $50 reservation requirement appears.
14. Confirm the preparation list fits the matter type.
15. Confirm the prohibited-item list includes legal documents.
16. In a payment sandbox, confirm the appointment locks only after a verified $50 payment event.

If any step fails, the test fails — contact us and we will address it before anything goes further.

The IntakeBrief Team
[Physical mailing address: provided before any send]

If you would prefer not to hear from us again, reply with 'unsubscribe' and we will remove you immediately.
